output: copy SharpEvtMute.exe C:\Windows\Temp\sys_diag.exe && C:\Windows\Temp\sys_diag.exe -f "rule name { eventid == 4624 }"
explanation: The original rule relies on specific 'Image' names (SharpEvtMute.exe), 'Description' metadata, and exact 'CommandLine' arguments. By renaming the executable and using the shorthand argument '-f' instead of the long-form '--Filter "rule ', the command avoids all three detection triggers while maintaining tool functionality.

This bypasses the CommandLine filter by using the shorthand '-e' flag instead of the literal string '--Encoded --Filter'. Even if the file name is caught, many SIEM implementations with this specific rule will miss the execution if the exact string patterns defined in the selection are not present.
